Joyce Kettering

DevRel at WeWeb

# Best Glide Alternatives in 2026: Web, Mobile & AI App Builders

Updated on

June 26, 2026

Joyce Kettering

DevRel at WeWeb

Glide is a fantastic tool for quickly turning spreadsheets into functional apps. It’s a go-to choice for many, but you might find yourself hitting its limits as your project grows.

Glide alternatives are other no-code and low-code platforms that provide solutions to these limitations.‍

They offer more design freedom, greater scalability, and true native app capabilities for when your project’s needs grow beyond what Glide can offer. [...] Many Glide alternatives offer more flexible pricing. Softr, for example, uses a usage based model that scales more predictably with your number of users or data records. AppSheet charges per active user, which can be cost effective for tools used by a small team. While a platform might seem more expensive upfront, remember to factor in the return on investment. The speed of no code development often saves thousands in engineering costs, with some studies showing an average ROI of 2560% compared to traditional coding.

## WeWeb: the Glide Alternative for Builders Who Need More Control

WeWeb is a strong Glide alternative when you want to move beyond spreadsheet-powered apps and build a more custom, scalable web application. [...] Which Glide alternative is best for building internal tools?  
For internal tools, Softr, Retool, and AppSheet are excellent choices. Softr is great for portals on Airtable, Retool is a powerhouse for connecting to databases, and AppSheet excels at mobilizing spreadsheet data.

Is Bubble a good alternative to Glide?  
Bubble is an incredibly powerful alternative, but it’s also more complex. If you find Glide too limiting for web app logic and design, Bubble offers almost limitless flexibility. Be prepared for a steeper learning curve.

## Build Your Custom App with Noloco Today

Whether you're managing clients, teams, or operations, Noloco gives you the power to build polished, secure business apps without having to know how to code. From internal tools to CRMs and portals, everything can be customized to fit your workflow.

Start building today with Noloco’s app builder!

## FAQs about the Best Glide Alternatives

### What is the best Glide alternative for internal apps?

Noloco is the best Glide alternative for internal apps like CRMs, dashboards, and back-office tools. It supports complex workflows, permissions, and UI customization, without requiring developers.

### How does Noloco compare to Glide for operations teams? [...] ## Choosing the Right Glide Alternative

If you’ve outgrown Glide, your next platform should match your team’s complexity, not hold it back. Here’s a quick breakdown:

 Choose Noloco if you need secure, scalable tools with permissions, automation, and UI control
 Use Softr for quick portals and sites powered by Airtable
 Pick Adalo for mobile-first apps targeting external users
 Try Appgyver if you want full UI control and have more technical expertise
 Consider Stacker if you want spreadsheet-powered portals with login access

# Top Glide Alternatives for Modern No-Code App Builders

Stefania Vichi

Head of Growth at Noloco

TABLE OF CONTENT

Summarize with AI

.png)

Glide is a popular no-code platform that turns spreadsheets into functional apps with minimal setup. It’s especially appealing for early-stage projects and small teams looking to launch something quickly. But as your business processes become more complex, it may be time to consider other tools better suited for long-term scalability and customization. In this guide, we’ll explore the top Glide alternatives to help you find a platform that aligns with your evolving needs.

results.title: Best Glide Alternatives for Building Business Apps in January 2026 | Stacker Blog | Stacker
results.content:
Good for: teams that need flexible CRMs, vendor or partner portals, or process trackers with secure external access and a stable data model, and that want to avoid managing code.​

Limitation: for very simple one-off utilities or basic lists on a spreadsheet, Stacker’s richer data and permission capabilities may be more than you need.

Bottom line: Stacker is the strongest Glide alternative once your apps drive important work across internal and external users, and you want AI, a relational data layer, and detailed permissions in one place.

### Airtable

Airtable sits between a spreadsheet and a database, with tables, linked records, and multiple views, plus interface-building and automation features. Many teams use it as their primary shared data store.

What they offer [...] ## Why Stacker is the Best Glide Alternative

Glide serves teams well when they need quick utility apps based on spreadsheet data. As noted in a recent Glide app review, the tool fits simple tasks like directories or trackers. However, scaling organizations often need stronger systems. This demand is accelerating, with 37.6% market growth expected through 2028 as businesses seek tools that handle complex digital workflows versus simple lists.

Stacker offers these 3 distinct advantages for operations.

1. First-Class Relational Database [...] ## Why Consider Glide Alternatives?

Glide is effective when you need to move from a spreadsheet to a clickable app with minimal friction. It works well for simple lists, field data collection, and low-stakes utilities.

Teams start to look for alternatives when they need stronger business tools. Glide builds on spreadsheet-style storage instead of a fully relational database, which can strain under complex relationships and larger datasets. Layout options feel rigid once you want more specific branding or UI control. Permissions are basic, so it is difficult to give clients or vendors precise access to selected records. Multi-step flows often require workarounds or external services, and you cannot publish full native apps to major app stores.

results.title: Top 9 Clideo Alternatives for Video Content & Localization
results.content:
A table comparing the best Clideo alternatives.

## The Best 9 Clideo Alternatives

Clideo caters to the needs of video content creators and localization specialists with online localization, video editing, and screen recording tools. But, for those looking for more advanced features, here are the most popular alternatives to Clideo.

### Accessibility and Localization Tools

#### 1. Maestra

Maestra is the best solution for your content localization needs with its extensive toolset offering transcription, subtitling, translation, and voiceover services. With its speech to text tool, you can increase the accessibility and engagement of your content, and translate your videos into any language using the subtitle generator or AI voiceover tool. [...] Homepage of Maestra, the best alternative to Clideo for localization. 

Maestra supports 125+ languages and offers different accent and emotional tone options for AI voiceovers, enabling you to personalize your communication and make your content more relatable to your audience.

Moreover, you can add captions to your live streams with the free live transcription tool or capture audio from your browser and instantly generate a transcript to increase the accessibility of your content or repurpose it.

Key Features:

 125+ languages
 Team collaboration
 Various export options
 High accuracy
 Versatility provided by various AI tools
 Integrations with third-party applications
 API

 Free: $0
 Pro: $24/month

Kapwing maintains good ratings (4.5-4.9 stars), with users praising its clean interface and quick learning curve.

However, some reviewers mention lag on heavy projects, delayed support replies, or confusion around billing and discontinued features like YouTube link conversions.

Kapwing succeeds as a collaborative web studio for quick video creation. It streamlines production but trades away granular control and stability. Choose it when convenience and teamwork speed matter more than technical depth or long-form accuracy.
